subject: Mathematics
overall_learning_objectives: Construct triangles using ASA and RHS criteria
Solve complex construction problems involving multiple steps
Analyze the interior angles of regular polygons to justify tilings
Explore the work of M.C. Escher and mathematical art

lesson_plan.title: ASA and RHS Constructions
topics_covered: Two angles and the side between them (ASA)
Checking for uniqueness of the construction
teaching_strategy: Demonstrating the 'swinging' arc for RHS hypotenuse
student_activities: Construct a right triangle with Hypotenuse 10 cm and Side 6 cm
Construct a triangle given $\angle A=40^{\circ}, \angle B=60^{\circ}$ and $AB=5$ cm
assessment: Error-analysis: 'Find what went wrong in this RHS drawing'

lesson_plan.title: The Geometry of Tilings
topics_covered: Why regular pentagons don't tile ($108^{\circ}$ doesn't divide $360^{\circ}$)
teaching_strategy: Slide show of complex tessellations (Escher style)
student_activities: Calculate interior angles of polygons from 3 to 8 sides
Design a semi-regular tiling using squares and octagons
Tiling challenge: 'Will a regular heptagon tile?'

teacher_tip: Show students that the only regular polygons that tile the plane by themselves are Equilateral Triangles, Squares, and Hexagons. Let them try to prove why using the $360^{\circ}$ rule!
